_comment: Template documenting the consolidated JSON the /system-report master skill assembles (introduced 2026-06-22). It runs the three existing reviews (/health-review, /performance-review, /ml-review) in report mode and synthesizes one executive report. The three sub-objects `health`, `performance`, `ml` carry each review's own response shape verbatim (see comms/schema/{health,performance,ml}_review_response.template.json — do NOT re-declare their fields here, a second copy drifts). The `consolidated` block is the cross-domain executive synthesis + the report-specific data (trade dossiers, market context, per-class PnL). This file is documentation, not a validated JSON Schema. Renderer: scripts/reports/render_system_report.py. Spec: docs/reports/system-report-DESIGN.md.
prior_report_id: RPT-... | null (first report, or no prior in this window class)

health._comment: Verbatim /health-review response — shape in comms/schema/health_review_response.template.json. Run in report mode (its individual send-ping is SUPPRESSED; the report sends one consolidated ping). `claude_channel_ping.delivered_via` = 'suppressed (system-report)'.

performance._comment: Verbatim /performance-review response — shape in comms/schema/performance_review_response.template.json. Run in report mode (individual ping suppressed). Its trade_decision_grades[] feed the consolidated.trade_dossiers[] join.

ml._comment: Verbatim /ml-review response — shape in comms/schema/ml_review_response.template.json. Run in report mode (individual ping suppressed).

consolidated._comment: Cross-domain executive synthesis + report-specific data not produced by any single review.
consolidated.roll_up_grade: healthy | caution | investigate — worst-of the three reviews' overall_assessment
consolidated.headline: <= 240 chars — one-paragraph 'what happened since the last report'

operator_priorities._comment: Top 3-5 prioritized actions distilled across all three reviews. Highest-severity first.
operator_priorities.title: <= 120 chars

cross_review_notes: free-form — patterns that span domains (e.g. health flags signal->order plumbing on BTCUSDT AND performance shows a rejection cluster on the same symbol)

pnl_by_class._comment: Real / paper / prop are NEVER blended (live-trade management contract P4). Each is computed over the window AND the prior equal-length window so the report can show the trend (better/worse). Prop is sourced from the prop journal (isolated tables), not `trades`. Null any value not derivable.

paper._comment: Same shape as `real`, computed over paper-account rows only.

prop._comment: From /api/bot/prop/* — fills + account-status, NOT the `trades` table. rule_distance is the cushion to the account-killer limits.

market_context._comment: One row per traded symbol (enumerated live via _discover_symbols, never hardcoded). Window OHLC + % change from /api/bot/candles so performance reads against market behavior. Null on a candle-fetch failure (do not fabricate a 0).
market_context.note: <= 160 chars — one-line regime read (trending up / chop / sold off, etc.)

trade_dossiers._comment: The reviewable-one-by-one per-trade decision map. ADAPTIVE DEPTH: full dossier per trade for window in {since-last, daily}; for {weekly, monthly} only `notable=true` outliers get the full dossier, the rest are summarized in pnl_by_class.per_strategy + a count in `dossier_coverage`. Joins /api/bot/trades/closed + /api/bot/order-packages (by linkedTradeId) + the performance review's A-F grade.
trade_dossiers.close_reason: tp | sl | manual | reconciler | other | null

claude_grade._comment: From the performance review (comms/claude_strategy_scores.jsonl, joined by order_package_id). null until graded.
claude_grade.grade: A | B | C | D | F | null
claude_grade.entry_quality: optimal | acceptable | late | early | should_skip | unknown
claude_grade.exit_quality: optimal | tp_appropriate | sl_appropriate | premature_exit | held_too_long | unknown
claude_grade.risk_management: correct | oversize | undersize | sl_too_tight | sl_too_wide | unknown

model_scores._comment: order_packages.model_scores — {model_id: {stage, score}} captured at signal time. null for pre-column rows.

trade_dossiers.signal_logic: the decision reasoning the bot recorded at signal time (order_packages.signal_logic), or null

dossier_coverage._comment: How the adaptive-depth rule resolved for this report.

backlog_summary._comment: Roll-up of the three review backlogs. MUST be computed from the backlog files via scripts/reports/backlog_counts.py --since <window_start> — NEVER hand-entered (the 2026-06-23 bug: a hand-built summary put the *total* in health.open ('132') and left performance/ml.open null -> '—', when the real open counts were knowable). open/total/resolved are exact from the files; drained = items resolved within the window (precise when resolved_at is a full ISO timestamp; day-granular when it is date-only). The renderer leads with 'open / total'.

tier3_proposals_pending._comment: Tier-3 items the reviews PROPOSED (never enacted) — surfaced so the operator sees the decision queue in one place.

monitoring._comment: The Monitoring section (2026-06-25): backlog items the review is actively WATCHING rather than acting on this run — things that need more time (soaking / awaiting data) or a decision (a gate is met / operator-gated). Distinct from operator_priorities (act now) and from review_coverage.backlog_drive.deferred (the audit trail): this is the human-readable 'what are we waiting on' list surfaced in the report. Curate from the three backlogs' open items whose deferral reason is soak/data/decision — NOT stale-doc or code-fix items (those are actionable, not monitored). category in {soaking, awaiting-data, awaiting-decision, verify}.
monitoring.detail: <= 200 chars — what it is + what it's waiting on
monitoring.next_check: <= 60 chars — the trigger/criterion that ends the wait (e.g. 'n>=30 closed', 'next IB reset', 'operator go') | null

review_coverage._comment: REQUIRED (the Review-coverage guard, 2026-06-23). Proves the SYSTEM REVIEW actually covered its mandate — strategy promotion, ML training health, soak progress, AND that it WORKED THE BACKLOGS DOWN (not just counted them) — not just the trade/health summary. A run with any of strategy_promotion / ml_training_health / soak_status / backlog_drive missing or empty must STOP and complete the work before rendering. Relay-blocked data is allowed only as an explicit 'unavailable: <reason>' string, never silently omitted.

strategy_promotion._comment: Per-strategy promotion/demotion stance vs each gate (KILL/DEMOTE_SHADOW/TUNE/HOLD/PROMOTE), from the ml/performance sub-reviews + /api/bot/strategies/{name}/review. List ready-to-promote + demote/kill candidates, or state 'all HOLD'.

ml_training_health._comment: Did training cycles run since the last review? dataset builds OK? any failing/stuck cycle? From /ml-review + the trainer relay.

soak_status._comment: One row per active soak (shadow models, conviction, exit-ladder). state ∈ accruing|stalled|gate_met. Flag any stalled or met-but-unactioned soak in flags_raised.

flags_raised: free-form — the loud flags this review surfaced (degrading strategy, stalled soak, gate met-but-unactioned, training failure). Empty ONLY if genuinely nothing is degrading — say so explicitly.

account_reachability._comment: REQUIRED (2026-06-29). Per-account up/down for EVERY declared-live broker account (mode:live + a probeable exchange — excludes the dry/shelved ib_live/oanda_practice and the API-less breakout_1). Sources: /api/diag/exchange_positions (positions=null => unreachable), the latch state runtime_logs/account_reachability_alert_state.json (account_reachability_alert.down_accounts()), and /api/bot/accounts/balances (api_ok). ANY down live account is a MANDATORY flags_raised[] entry that fires its OWN standalone high-priority ping — never buried only in the report body. Motivated by the IB gateway going dark across reviews unflagged.
account_reachability.summary: <= 240 chars — 'all N live accounts reachable' or the down set | 'unavailable: <reason>'

backlog_drive._comment: REQUIRED. Proves the three review backlogs (docs/claude/{health,performance,ml}-review-backlog.json) were WORKED, not just counted. Per domain: which item ids were drained (resolved) this run, and which were deferred + why (each open item must be genuinely soaking / blocked-on-future-data / Tier-3-awaiting-operator — 'no time' / 'didn't look' is not valid). If drained is empty, `summary` must justify why every open item is non-actionable now.
backlog_drive.summary: <= 240 chars — what got worked down this run + why the rest is parked

artifacts._comment: Where this report was written. Relative to repo root; the VM's ict-git-sync mirrors comms/ so /api/bot/reports serves them. dashboard_link is the link the Telegram ping carries (opens the report IN the Streamlit app); github_link is a secondary reference (private repo → source only).

claude_channel_ping._comment: The ONE consolidated ping for the whole report (the three sub-reviews' individual pings are suppressed). Posted via the send-ping system-action. The link is artifacts.dashboard_link (the Streamlit Reports deep link), NOT the GitHub blob.
